world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 16
major_event: Ironmaul asked Varek one question before midday: 'Did you open it?' Varek looked at the iron case on the floor for a long moment and said, 'It opened for me.' Ironmaul did not ask again. By afternoon, the Monastery instruction rider arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate to find it sealed from the inside.

primary_event.name: Varek Speaks — 'It Opened For Me'
primary_event.summary: Before midday, Ironmaul asked Varek whether he had opened the iron case. Varek looked at it for a long time and said: 'It opened for me.' Ironmaul did not ask again. The case remains on the garrison floor, sealed. The Monastery instruction rider arrived at the outer gate at dusk and found it sealed from the inside. He is not being admitted.

supporting_events.name: Roderic Signs — The Proclamation Exists
supporting_events.summary: Before dawn, a palace physician confirmed to the attending steward that King Roderic Thorne II had pressed his seal to a document. The document was carried by Caeris himself to the palace archivist before breakfast. The proclamation of succession is now a legal instrument. No public announcement has been made.

supporting_events.name: Dur Khadur Reaches Four Miles — The City Now Knows
supporting_events.summary: The Dur Khadur advance unit made camp four miles from the Twin Cities harbor approach before dawn. A patrol rider from the city garrison spotted the camp at sunrise and reported to the garrison captain. By midmorning the captain had informed the palace. The Twin Cities knows. No one has yet connected it to the Admiral's declaration.

supporting_events.name: Shadow Court Envoy Meets Caeris — After the Signing
supporting_events.summary: The Nythariel envoy reached Caeris's private anteroom near midnight. He was admitted. Caeris had already sealed the proclamation. The envoy's offer of Shadow Court recognition arrived after the fact. Caeris listened, said little, and did not refuse. The terms were left open. Lythara does not yet know she was late.

active_events.name: Varek Speaks — 'It Opened For Me'
active_events.summary: Ironmaul asked his question. Varek's answer has produced more dread than silence did. The iron case remains sealed on the garrison floor. The Monastery rider is outside a locked gate. Whatever passed between Varek and the deep thing is inside the case — or inside Varek.
active_events.consequences: The Monastery instruction rider will attempt communication by morning. If Ironmaul admits him, the Monastery gains direct access to Varek. If not, Stonebreak may escalate to the senior druid observer deployment. The case's second seal must be verified — if it was breached and resealed, the world above has already been changed.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Exists — Succession Race Becomes Political Crisis
active_events.summary: The sealed document reached the palace archivist before breakfast. Caeris is now the legal heir. But Dur Khadur is four miles away and the garrison is alarmed. The Shadow Court envoy left the meeting with open terms. Every faction now has a different fragment of the picture.
active_events.consequences: If Caeris announces publicly before Dur Khadur is neutralized or explained, the military presence will overshadow the legal act. If he waits, the garrison pressure may force him to act on security first — legitimizing Dur Khadur's presence before he can contest it.

active_events.name: Dur Khadur Forward Unit — Four Miles, City Aware
active_events.summary: The camp at four miles is now known to the Twin Cities garrison. The captain has briefed the palace. No one in the city has yet received the Admiral's declaration or knows the unit moves under a retainer clause. The unit looks like a military incursion.
active_events.consequences: The Twin Cities may mobilize garrison forces toward the harbor approach within 1 tick unless an explanation arrives. The Admiral's continued isolation makes that explanation structurally impossible. The Dur Khadur commander has no instruction to advance further without second authorization — but the posture alone is explosive.

active_events.name: Admiral — Isolation and Collapse Risk
active_events.summary: The Admiral has been behind his study door through a full night and day. His prophet managed to pass a note under the door by morning; it was not returned. The council, now aware that the Admiral has not attended two consecutive sessions, convened without him. The deputy admiral was asked to chair. He declined.
active_events.consequences: If the Admiral does not appear within one more tick, the council will formally convene without him — a constitutional first for Tidefall. The prophet's silence from inside the study is now more alarming than the door itself. If the Admiral is incapacitated or has harmed himself, Tidefall's entire naval command structure becomes contested.

active_events.name: Rowen at the Ruin — The Submerged Layer
active_events.summary: Rowen's shallow-draft boat reached the submerged stonework and he and the navigator spent the morning in diving and rope work. By midday Rowen had brought up two items: a stone tablet fragment covered in a script no one on the fleet recognized, and a sealed bronze tube corroded but intact. He returned to the ship before dusk. He has not shown either item to the crew.
active_events.consequences: The bronze tube and stone tablet are now aboard Rowen's flagship. If the script is the same as that on the ancient document that sent him here, the ruin is confirmed as connected. The tablet and tube together may constitute the next instruction — or the next warning.

active_events.name: Velorn Clan Consolidation — Shadow Court Fracture
active_events.summary: While Lythara focused on the Twin Cities, Velorn secured a fourth minor house and Arkenor's lieutenant attended as an informal observer. The coalition is now substantial enough to be called one. Lythara still does not know the scale of what has assembled in her absence.
active_events.consequences: When Lythara's attention finally returns inward — likely after the Twin Cities play resolves — she will find a reorganized internal opposition with Arkenor's informal blessing. If she moved against Velorn now it would be visible and costly. If she waits, the coalition hardens.

active_events.name: Monastery Instruction Rider — Locked Out
active_events.summary: The instruction rider arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate at dusk and found it sealed. He called out through the gate slot. A guard responded that no visitors were being admitted by order of the Thane. The rider has camped at the road's edge and sent a fast return message to Stonebreak: 'Gate sealed. Varek status unknown. Awaiting instruction.'
active_events.consequences: Stonebreak will receive the rider's report within 1-2 ticks. The senior druid observer deployment, already in preparation, will now be authorized. The Monastery is about to escalate from instruction to oversight — and Ironmaul may read that as an intrusion.

active_events.name: Vilefin Tinker-Informant — Package Complete
active_events.summary: The tinker-informant observed the Monastery rider being turned away from the outer gate. He now has: supply absence confirmed, extra garrison post, iron frame unfinished, outer gate sealed to a known Monastery rider. The intelligence package is complete and highly valuable. He will depart for buyers before dawn.
active_events.consequences: Within 1-2 ticks the Vilefin intelligence will reach the Monastery, the Gloomspire Gnomes, and potentially the Shadow Court — each receiving the same facts through different filters. The gate-sealing of a Monastery rider is explosive information in the right hands.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul asked Varek one question before midday. Varek's answer — 'It opened for me' — ended the conversation. Ironmaul ordered the outer gate sealed at midday, and when the Monastery rider arrived at dusk, the gate guard told him to camp and wait. Ironmaul has not explained to any garrison member what this means. He sat with the iron case through the afternoon, not touching it. Cost: 3 influence (gate seal, garrison control), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul does not know what Varek contacted. He knows the case came back sealed. He knows what Varek said. He is not letting the Monastery in until he understands what he is protecting them from — or protecting Varek from them.
faction_actions.target: Garrison interior — sealed access, iron case observed, Monastery rider excluded

faction_actions.action: The instruction rider's fast return message was prepared at dusk. Meanwhile at Stonebreak, the council formally authorized the senior druid observer — Elder Sondra Ashveil — to travel north as override authority with full dispensation to assess and if necessary contain Varek. She will depart at dawn. Cost: 4 gold (authorization session, Ashveil travel preparation), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The gate seal confirms Ironmaul is holding something back. The council's fear has crystallized: if Varek opened the case or was opened by it, the containment protocol must be administered by someone with the authority to act, not just instruct.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld — Elder Ashveil dispatched north with override authority

faction_actions.action: The Tidefall council convened without the Admiral by midmorning. The deputy admiral declined to chair; the senior trade councillor ran the session. Two items were raised: the Admiral's absence and a report of unusual rider movement on the southern road. No connection to Dur Khadur was made. The prophet slid a second note under the study door before noon. No response. Cost: 2 influence (unauthorized council session), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The institution is beginning to function around its missing head. This is either responsible governance or the first stage of a quiet coup, depending on who is watching and from where.
faction_actions.target: Admiral's study — second note, no response; council session without him

faction_actions.action: The advance unit commander held position at four miles. No further advance. He received no second authorization from the Dark Council or the Admiral. He sent a coded query back to the Dark Council: retainer clause active, position held, city garrison aware — requesting clarification on whether to continue advisory posture or begin visible withdrawal for plausible deniability. Cost: 3 military (unit maintenance), 2 gold (courier).
faction_actions.reason: The commander is professional and disciplined. He moved when authorized and stopped when uncertain. The Dark Council has not been formally briefed yet — his query may be the first they hear of the full situation.
faction_actions.target: Dark Council — coded query on next instruction; position held at four miles

faction_actions.action: The garrison captain briefed Caeris directly — not the aging king — about the Dur Khadur camp at four miles. Caeris, who holds the signed proclamation, ordered the captain to maintain elevated readiness but make no movement toward the camp. He then sent a sealed letter to the Tidefall admiral's office asking formally for 'clarification on the nature and authorization of forces currently observed near the harbor approach.' Cost: 4 gold (garrison readiness, formal dispatch), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris has the proclamation. He does not want his succession to begin with a military engagement. He is trying to force Tidefall to explain the unit before he has to act against it — buying time and establishing legal standing simultaneously.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall — formal clarification demand; garrison on elevated readiness, not yet mobilized

faction_actions.action: Caeris told the Shadow Court envoy, just before midnight, that he was 'not ungrateful for the Faerwood's interest' and that 'certain understandings between neighbors might be worth exploring after a period of stability.' He gave nothing concrete. He kept the envoy at arm's length but did not close the door. In the morning he visited his father again — Roderic was asleep and did not wake. Cost: 3 influence (envoy meeting, political navigation), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is playing the only hand he has: delay everyone who wants something from him until his position is uncontestable. The Shadow Court can be useful later. Right now they are a pressure he cannot afford to convert into an enemy.
faction_actions.target: Shadow Court envoy — open non-commitment; King Roderic — morning visit, no response

faction_actions.action: The Nythariel envoy sent Lythara a coded report by Shadow Road at dawn: 'Document already signed before I arrived. Caeris received offer — terms not refused, not accepted. Window for influence open, not wide.' Lythara received it by midmorning. She is now facing Velorn's gathering, a late Twin Cities bid, and Arkenor's informal alignment — all at once. She called a full clan council for three days hence. Cost: 5 gold (Shadow Road, clan council preparation), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara knows she was late. She does not know how late. She is trying to consolidate before the next move — but calling a clan council signals to Velorn that she is aware of pressure without yet knowing its full shape.
faction_actions.target: Internal — clan council called; Velorn coalition partially visible to Lythara now

faction_actions.action: The Shadow Court envoy's Shadow Road dispatch was intercepted in transit by a Gnome courier relay node — not decrypted, but its existence and timing noted. The Gnomes sent the pre-prepared coded dispatch to the Shadow Court envoy directly, now in Twin Cities, offering the Tidefall declaration intelligence for 12 gold. They also passed a summary of the Dur Khadur camp position to Glenhaven's formal envoy contact for 8 gold. Cost: 3 gold (courier, relay node operation), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnomes are selling the same crisis to multiple buyers at different angles. Glenhaven buys military intelligence. The Shadow Court buys political intelligence. Neither buyer knows the Gnomes are selling to both.
faction_actions.target: Shadow Court envoy and Glenhaven envoy contact — parallel intelligence sale

faction_actions.action: The Vampire operative in Twin Cities confirmed the proclamation was signed and sent a brief dispatch to the Lythara network: 'Document sealed before your man arrived. Caeris holds the instrument. Dur Khadur four miles out. Window is now, not later.' The operative also approached the palace physician with a small social gift and a question about the king's health — not medical, but temporal. How long does Roderic have? Cost: 4 influence (dual dispatch, physician approach), 2 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires want to know whether Caeris will consolidate quickly or remain vulnerable during a transition period. If Roderic dies soon, there will be a raw interval. That interval is when pressure is most effective.
faction_actions.target: Lythara network — updated dispatch; Palace physician — longevity inquiry

faction_actions.action: Elowen received the Gnome-sourced intelligence about the Dur Khadur camp position by afternoon. She convened a second closed council. The formal envoy request to Tidefall was upgraded to an urgent diplomatic query. A Wayfarer messenger was sent toward the Twin Cities bearing Glenhaven's offer of 'neutral facilitation' between any parties in dispute. Cost: 3 gold (Gnome payment, Wayfarer dispatch, council session), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen sees an opportunity to position Glenhaven as a stabilizing voice before the military situation crystallizes. Neutral facilitation is both principled and strategically useful — it gains access to all parties without committing to any.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities and Tidefall — neutral facilitation offer; Gnome intelligence purchased

faction_actions.action: Rowen returned to the flagship with the bronze tube and stone tablet. He spent the evening with the navigator comparing the tablet's script against the margins of the ancient document. By midnight, the navigator confirmed: the script matched marginalia in the document's final three pages — the pages Rowen had not shown the crew. Rowen ordered the fleet to hold anchor through the next day. Cost: 1 military (anchor watch), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The confirmation changes the nature of the expedition. Rowen is no longer following a lead — he has arrived at the source. The bronze tube is sealed and he has not yet opened it. He is deciding whether he wants to know what is inside.
faction_actions.target: Bronze tube and stone tablet — script confirmed matching; fleet anchored, holding

faction_actions.action: Farrock's commander received no further communication from Lostfeld and took that silence as confirming the emergency protocol. He quietly increased the garrison watch rotation and drafted a preliminary message to the Twin Cities — not sent yet — asking whether any unusual activity had been observed in the northern passes. Cost: 1 gold (watch rotation adjustment), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock is not alarmed enough to act but alarmed enough to prepare. The silence from Lostfeld after an emergency protocol message is the precise condition the protocol was designed for.
faction_actions.target: Internal — watch increased; Twin Cities preliminary message drafted but held

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant packed his camp before dawn and departed Lostfeld's outer road heading south. He carries an intelligence package: supply blackout confirmed, sealed garrison, iron frame unfinished, Monastery rider turned away. He will reach the nearest Vilefin market post by tomorrow. From there the package enters the bidding network. Cost: 1 gold (supplies, travel).
faction_actions.reason: The informant is professional and has what he came for. Staying longer risks attention. The package is worth more if he leaves before the situation changes — which it clearly will.
faction_actions.target: Vilefin market post — intelligence package delivery, auction to highest bidder

faction_actions.action: The apprentice shaman's bead-message arrived at the Gilgeth elder council before midday. The elders assembled. The message described military formation movement south of the Twin Cities road. Elder Councilor Mrak, the senior shaman, interpreted the movement as 'the crowned storm stepping sideways before it falls.' The council dispatched a war-drum message to the nearest Gilgeth tribal post: 'Eyes open. No caravans south for three days.' Cost: 2 gold (council session, drum runner), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gilgeth elders do not have enough information to act militarily, but enough to protect their trade assets. Suspending southbound caravans is cheap and buys intelligence time.
faction_actions.target: Gilgeth tribal network — caravan suspension south; eyes-open posture

recent_events.text: Varek told Ironmaul the iron case 'opened for him.' Ironmaul sealed the outer gate. The Monastery instruction rider arrived at dusk and was turned away. He camps at the road's edge. The iron frame stands unfinished in the cold.

King Roderic Thorne II pressed his seal to the succession document before dawn. Caeris delivered it to the palace archivist at breakfast. No public announcement. By midmorning the garrison captain reported a Dur Khadur camp four miles from the harbor approach.

The Nythariel shadow-envoy reached Caeris after midnight. He found the document already sealed and the terms already Caeris's. The meeting ended without commitment. Lythara was told by dawn: she arrived late.

The Admiral has not emerged from his study. The council convened without him for the first time. No deputy would chair. The prophet's second note went unanswered. The city does not know the retainer clause exists.

Rowen returned from the submerged layer with a bronze tube and a stone tablet. His navigator confirmed the tablet's script matches the ancient document's marginalia. The fleet holds anchor. The bronze tube remains sealed.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Caeris vs Dur Khadur Presence
active_tensions.description: Caeris holds a signed proclamation and has demanded formal explanation from Tidefall. Dur Khadur sits four miles from the harbor approach. The garrison is on elevated readiness. The Admiral cannot provide the explanation because he is locked in his study. The window before military escalation is narrowing.

Ironmaul has sealed the gate against the Monastery's instruction rider. Elder Ashveil is departing Stonebreak at dawn with override authority. Varek is inside with the iron case. Whatever Varek knows or has become, the Monastery is coming to take jurisdiction — and Ironmaul may not let them.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Leaderless Council and Admiral Collapse
active_tensions.description: The Admiral triggered a foreign military movement and vanished. His council met without him. His prophet cannot reach him. If he is incapacitated, Tidefall has no one who can explain or retract the retainer clause — and no one who can authorize Dur Khadur to stand down.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Lythara Behind in Three Simultaneous Plays
active_tensions.description: Late to the Twin Cities signing, blind to Velorn's coalition scale, and now calling a clan council that signals awareness without authority. Lythara is reacting to events she did not shape. Each day of reaction costs credibility she cannot easily rebuild.

character_updates.name: Grand Druid Varek
character_updates.status: Told Ironmaul 'it opened for me.' Has spoken no further. Still seated in the sealed garrison. Eyes remain altered. The iron case is on the floor. He has eaten but not slept. He watches the case.

character_updates.name: Thane Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Sealed the outer gate after Varek's answer. Turned away the Monastery rider personally through the gate slot. Sat with the case through the afternoon without touching it. Has told no one what Varek said.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Holds the signed proclamation. Managing the Shadow Court envoy, garrison alarm, and Tidefall query simultaneously. Has not made the succession public. Visited his father at morning — Roderic did not wake.

character_updates.name: Admiral (Tidefall)
character_updates.status: Second full day behind a locked study door. Council has convened without him. Prophet's two notes unanswered. His physical condition is unknown. The retainer clause has no one to revoke it.

character_updates.name: Rowen
character_updates.status: Returned to the flagship with bronze tube and stone tablet. Script confirmed matching ancient document. Has not opened the tube. Fleet holds anchor. He slept four hours and is awake again, studying the tablet.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Received word she was late to the signing. Knows Caeris left terms open. Has called a clan council in three days, which signals awareness of internal pressure without naming Velorn directly. Velorn has already heard of the council call.

faction_morale.reason: Varek's three words have removed all remaining comfort. The garrison knows something was said. They do not know what. The sealed gate has turned the fortress inward on itself. Ironmaul is unreachable by normal channels.

The Admiral is behind a locked door for a second full day. The council met without him. The prophet is frightened. The city does not know foreign troops are four miles from an allied city because their Admiral whispered them there.

The succession document exists but is secret. The Dur Khadur camp is four miles out and real. Caeris is holding it together through delay and formal language while the garrison watches the southern road.

The gate was sealed to their rider. Elder Ashveil departs at dawn. The council at Stonebreak is now in a controlled panic — fear has converted to resolve, but the resolve is aimed at a man they may not be able to contain.
Lythara arrived late to the signing, is behind on Velorn, and has called a clan council that signals weakness as much as leadership. Three plays running simultaneously and none fully in her hand.
Unit held at four miles as ordered. Commander is professional and patient. Coded query sent to the Dark Council. Waiting for instruction with military discipline intact. No panic, no overreach.

Two intelligence sales executed or in motion. Shadow Road intercept noted. The Gnomes are profiting from every actor's partial knowledge. The more uncertain the world becomes, the more valuable their position.

The ruin yielded what it promised. Rowen has the objects. The fleet holds anchor. The bronze tube is the only remaining question. Morale among the crew is curious rather than anxious — Rowen found something real.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
